results.title: The Flinders: ranges of legend
results.creator_credit: G Shepherd and S A Government Tourist Bureau
results.brief_description: The geological origin and the place of the Flinders Range in Aboriginal mythology. Shows rock paintings and carvings in the Wilpena area, the old mining town of Blinman and the ruins of Kanyaka.

results.title: Flight: a new awareness
results.brief_description: As young people fly with hang gliders and a pilot soars upwards in a glider plane, the pilot reflects on how despite the earth’s many wonders - humans have always envied the birds and longed to fly. Soaring higher and higher, the pilot experiences an exhilarating freedom and a whole new perspective of the earth below. The earth becomes a single, living organism - more beautiful than he ever imagined. He discovers how fragile a hold life has on this planet and dreams of a technology that refines and perfects, rather than destroys and pollutes.

results.title: Flight plan
results.creator_credit: Stanley Hawes and Australian National Film Board
results.brief_description: The organisation of ciial aviation in Australia; the planning and control behind, and during, every flight; the training of pilots and maintenance staff; plans for the future development of the service. Also features the Royal Flying Doctor Service.

results.title: The Flight of the Gossamer Condor
results.creator_credit: Ben Shedd, Jacqueline Shedd and Shedd Productions
results.brief_description: The story of how Dr. Paul MacCready won the Kemer Prize for the first man-powered aircraft that could fly a mile long figure of eight course. Follows the inventor through twelve months of planning and disappointments as he develops his idea from the first crude wing into a sophisticated craft that flies the course.

results.title: The Flight of the dragon
results.creator_credit: Leslie Thatcher, Graham McInnes and National Film Board of Canada
results.brief_description: Bronzes of the Han and Wei dynasties, T’ang ceramics, Sung water colors and Buddhist frescoes of the Yuan all express in some way the Chinese philosophy of life. These glimpses of the art of China gives us some conception of this age-old civilisation. Dr. Liu Shih Shun, the then Chinese ambassador to Canada, narrates highlights in the history of the country, as a parade of her delicate masterpieces, drawn from the famous collection in the Royal Ontario Museum, passes before the camera.
results.creator_credit_for_label: Leslie Thatcher, Graham McInnes and National Film Board of Canada

results.title: Flight of Apollo 7
results.creator_credit: Hearst Metrotone News and United States. National Aeronautics Space Administration
results.brief_description: Many remarkable and memorable shots of the Apollo 7 moon test flight which took place in October 1968. Shows the three astronauts Walter Schirra, Donn Eisele and Walter Cunningham in their cabin while in orbit.

results.title: Flight in white
results.creator_credit: William Canning and National Film Board of Canada
results.brief_description: Nothing a man does on his own brings him closer to flight than skiing, a sport for which Canada provides the ultimate facilities. Filmed in 1968 in Banff and Jasper National Parks in the famed Canadian Rockies, where the alpine scenery of winter is no less spectacular than that of summer, this picture reveals the fine blend of exhilaration, risk, physical prowess and, of course, speed, that are all part of this challenging sport. Film without words.

results.title: Flight: an anthology
results.brief_description: An anthology of verse, prose, music and film about flying. In an age of technicalities and super-sonic developments, the film recaptures the basic freedom of getting above the clouds.

results.title: Flax
results.brief_description: Opens with scenes of the flax industry in pre-war Ireland. With the outbreak of war, Britain’s supply of European flax was cut off. The film shows various improvements adopted to increase the production of flax in Britain, including the use of machines to pull the fibre and also the use of material in the unretted state.
